title: Theory Unit 01 Dummy System Berserk
wikitext:
[]: Theory: Unit 01 + Dummy System = Berserk? (SPOILERS!)             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Sun Aug 16, 2009 8:11 pm This is something that's been irking at me ever since I watched Episode 18 of Evangelion. In the episode, after the dummy system is activated, Unit-01 begins to furiously destroy Bardiel/ 03 under the machines' influence. This is plausible in that the dummy system does not feel human emotion or anything of that nature, but what really got me was this: During the fight, Unit- 01 is shown prying open the jaw restraints placed upon the Eva, a trait shown twice before that in the series preceded by the Evangelion going berserk. So my question was this: Why would being under the influence of the dummy system, made to replicate the actions of an Eva pilot cause the Eva to shred the restraints like that? I wasn't able to formulate an answer to this question until I saw Evangelion 2.0. Unit 01 roarin', under the influence of the dummy system, from one of the HD 2.0 trailers: In 2.0, Unit 01, under the influence of the dummy system once again tears Unit 03 to pieces. However, it wasn't EXACTLY the same as it was in episode 18. In 2.0, not only did the dummy system shred the jaw restraints, it ALSO screamed loudly as if it was berserk, like it did in 1.0 during the Sachiel fight and in the original series when it was fighting foes such as Leliel and Zeruel. It also should be noted it ripped apart Unit 03 at some organs with its TEETH, in positions I found ridiculously similar to episode 19 when Zeruel was devoured: So in this case, I think its appropriate to label the dummy's influence on the Eva in 2.0 as semi-berserk or something along those lines. Here's why. In 2.0, its evident that the pilots can force the Eva into going berserk on their own by reaching dangerously high synch levels, demonstrated by both Mari and Shinji during the Zeruel fight in the movie, AND by Asuka in End of Evangelion. This is not looked upon as an efficent strategy by NERV because not only does the Eva reach levels of power beyond human control, it also tangifies the pilots. Now we know the pilots CAN willingly force an Eva into going berserk. The dummy plug system can replicate the pilots actions using Reis' thoughts. So heres my point: Because the dummy system is a proxy pilot, fooling it into thinking its being piloted by one of the selected children, there wouldn't be any downside to having the Eva go berserk under the influence of the dummy system because its both under control by NERV AND, if the synch between the pilot and the Eva is cut (as Gendo had Maya do before the system activation), it wouldn't be able to turn the pilot into goo. Therefore, TLDR version, I believe the Dummy System in 2.0 allows the Evangelion to go into a sort of "semi-berserk mode," of course much different from the form Unit 01 takes when it battles Zer. This is my first real "theory" post. Tell me what you think, and if there are any errors, please correct me.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Sun Aug 16, 2009 9:08 pm That was really in-depth, but I've always just assumed that the Dummy System causing a berserker state was a given. As you pointed out, the broken jaw restraints were a dead give-away.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 16, 2009 9:17 pm Rebuild doesn't really change anything in the "fan theory" department. Dummy system was always speculated as instigating the Eva into a "semi-pseudo-berserk-something-or-other" state. Whether or not that's accurate is something else, and a question probably better suited for Discussion (since this conversation would inevitably end up being mostly about the original series, I wager). Only real difference is that the Zeruel munchies scene was dropped and some aspects of it were siphoned into the Eva-03 maiming scene to compensate for the loss (along with pumping up the violence level for the cinema, of course).

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Mon Aug 17, 2009 6:34 am How about doing it with rebuild only? I would like to see it this way: Bosou condition is roughly defined as "Eva controls pilot", denoted by the bridge staff telling us the "pulses are flowing backwards." Broken restraints and armor are most directly explained by "Eva reverting to type", and seems to accompany the use of power beyond the design limits of human external control. They can occur at the same time, and we can see them separately. I wonder if you agree? This seems to be taken from cannon to me. I can also groundlessly speculate that the dummy plug winds up being a copy of Rei's mind during her meditation in LCL tank, but without the life experience engrams and soul to control it. Under these conditions, only the stimulus/response of her unconscious mind determines the resulting personality. Since her emotional life with Gendo and Ritsuko would likely kill a lesser being before even 10 years in the program, this person is not ... pleasant. Lots of vivisection and bio-computing techniques allow NERV to dictate certain parts of the cyborg, such as designating what to attack, when to raise the AT field, etc. But such control is not implemented for the emotional parts which remain, of necessity, in the donor brain. So the attack command would be a 'trip down the ladder', a spiral of unreasoning hate mixed with fear succeeding to demands for more power (higher synch) and physical actions of primitive overkill to gratify to drive. There seems to be a way to switch control back after the dummy system is turned off, but berserker basically resists external efforts to stop it's rampage. >whew< @Reichu - Probably no 'munchies' in 2.0 for two reasons. Shinji in RoE seems full of adult-like rage and schadenfreude with Zeruel, not a full collapse to the brainstem like Sachiel. Though using a bad strategy, I thought the berserk team wanted to kill, then eat his core in 1.0. Only experience can teach that it must be done [chiller] alive. [/chiller] Thus Zeruel gets his core emptied out and Ayanami removed first, then killed. Unit 01 would seem to have this knowledge, as Shinji's control inputs are shown to be a vaguely conforming heroic notion, all the details are left to Yui-sama. Second is the possibility that unit 01 doesn't really need anything from an angel this time.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Aug 17, 2009 12:11 pm i personally thought the dummy plug control in rebuild was something closer to mari's "beast mode" than to an actual berserker. as in, yes, there's a certain degree of freedom and animalistic instinct (they open their jaw) but something/somebody else is still controlling the EVA so there's not complete freedom. it's not just the soul within taking over and starting the carnage. (also, note the similarities in the restraint of the person/thing controlling them-- mari doesn't particularly care for normal "human" propriety, and the dummy plug doesn't have a soul so it's technically not fully human). this is just me, but that's basically the main difference in my mind: the control. behavior-wise, they're probably pretty much the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Aug 17, 2009 12:43 pm For me dummy plug forced a berserk mode but under normal berserk conditions, more primal mommy love was there as the eva wasnt being lied to

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Mon Aug 17, 2009 10:32 pm I would say, Dummy is Dummy. It may simply be able to do somethings that some pilot can't, I mean like, when she activated ZA BEASTO, Mari got Unit 02's mouth to open right open and it reacted to Mari's emotions with different growls and roars. Now I see this as a sign that the pilot is very skilled. Let's talk about it this way; Regular pilots make Evas move and fight, the real good pilot can use some pretty fancy maneuvers and some tricks, and if the pilot is exceedingly good, can take control of more than the Eva's limbs and body but also use the jaw as well. How good the pilot is depends on their mental state and this can vary quite a bit based on conflicts in or outside the Eva. The Dummy however does not have a mental condition, it simply takes orders and it's "mental state" or whatever the Dummy has stays constant, Im guessing at a high level and can use the Jaw.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Aug 17, 2009 10:39 pm When I saw the original episode the dummy plug activation only came to me as a semi-berserk mode or something.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Tue Aug 18, 2009 8:10 am If i remember correctly, the dummy system is not controlled, it works independently. so therefore, we may not say that NERV has control over it, they just have the capability of starting it but not the ability of piloting it.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Tue Aug 18, 2009 2:32 pm So what if the Dummy plug works indepentantly, would it be possible that it could actually develop itself, what Im getttin at is that computers work by us telling them what to do and prgramming them right? So if the dummy is simply programmed then Gendo says GFTO there an beat dat Angel, could it not also develop a logic of it's own?

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Tue Aug 18, 2009 11:23 pm In my theory, the dummy plug works as if it has a logic of its own. Have you ever heard of "Fuzzy Logic" ? It may be implied that the dummy plug uses this system in order for it to operate, and decide for itself. For further readings... Fuzzy Logic

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Tue Aug 18, 2009 11:53 pm S'gatta be Fuzzy Logic, unlike a lot of other topics we've had, I think this one is completed. This makes perfect sense.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Thu Aug 20, 2009 4:16 am It may be, or may be not. We just need the others to say what they've got to say about this, and by others I mean the gods (reichu, etc.) and goddesses (LiLi[?] , etc.) of this forum.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Aug 20, 2009 7:45 am trueno_twentyone wrote: by others I mean the gods (reichu, etc.) and goddesses (LiLi[?] , etc.) of this forum.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Thu Aug 20, 2009 11:31 am This is off the record: I really never had the time to read other people's sigs. Sorry if i categorized you the wrong way don't ban me for doing so...please? I'll be more careful next time.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Aug 20, 2009 1:08 pm Chill out, friend, she's not going to ban someone over a silly, honest mista-*banned for painting Reichu as a sympathetic figure with HOO-MAN emotion circuits*

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Thu Aug 20, 2009 11:23 pm trueno_twentyone wrote: It may be, or may be not. We just need the others to say what they've got to say about this, and by others I mean the gods (reichu, etc.) and goddesses (LiLi[?] , etc.) of this forum.
